author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew III. 7.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees come to his baptism, he said unto them, O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then doth Christ say, that they did not believe John?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because this was not believing, to decline receiving Him whom he preached.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so they thought they regarded their prophets and their lawgiver, nevertheless He said they had not regarded them, forasmuch as they received not Him, that was foretold by them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For if ye had believed Moses,” saith He, “ye would have believed Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And after this again, being asked by Christ, “The baptism of John, whence is it?”they said, “If we shall say, Of earth, we fear the people; if we shall say, From heaven, He will say unto us, How then did ye not believe him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that from all these things it is manifest that they came indeed and were baptized, yet they did not abide in the belief of that which was preached.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For John also points out their wickedness, by their sendingunto the Baptist, and saying, “Art thou Elias?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Art thou Christ?” wherefore he also added, “they which were sent were of the Pharisees.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “What then? were not the multitudes also of this same mind”? one may say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, the multitudes in simplicity of mind had this suspicion, but the Pharisees, wishing to lay hold of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since it was acknowledged that Christ comes out of the village of David, and this man was of the tribe of Levi, they laid a snare by the question, in order that if he should say any such thing they might quickly come upon him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This at any rate he hath declared by what follows; for on his not acknowledging any of the things which they expected, even so they take hold of him, saying, “Why baptizest thou then, if thou be not the Christ?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to convince thee that the Pharisees came with one mind, and the people with another, hear how the evangelist hath declared this too; saying of the people, “that they came and were baptized of him, confessing their sins;”but concerning the Pharisees, no longer like that, but that “when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming, he said, O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: O greatness of mind!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How doth he discourse unto men ever thirsting after the blood of the prophets, and in disposition no better than serpents! how doth he disparage both themselves and their progenitors with all plainness!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Yea,” saith one; “he speaks plainly enough, but the question is if there be any reason in this plainness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he did not see them sinning, but in the act of change; wherefore they did not deserve blame, but rather praise and approbation, for having left city and houses, and making haste to hear his preaching.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then shall we say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That he had not things present, and even now doing, in his view, but he knew the secrets of their mind, God having revealed this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since then they were priding themselves on their forefathers, and this was like to prove the cause of their destruction, and was casting them into a state of carelessness, he cuts away the roots of their pride.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause Isaiah also calls them, “rulers of Sodom,” and “people of Gomorrah;”and another prophet saith, “Are ye not as children of the Ethiopians;”and all withdraw them from this way of thinking, bringing down their pride, which had caused them unnumbered evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But the prophets,” you will say, “naturally did so; for they saw them sinning: but in this case, with what view and for what cause doeth he the same, seeing them obey him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To make them yet more tender-hearted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if one accurately mark his words, he hath also tempered his rebuke with commendation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he spake these things, as marveling at them, that they were become able, however late, to do what seemed almost an impossibility for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: His rebuke, you see, is rather that of one bringing them over, and working upon them to arouse themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in that he appears amazed, he implies both their former wickedness to be great, and their conversion marvellous and beyond expectation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, “what hath come to pass,” saith he, “that being children of those men, and brought up so badly, they have repented?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence hath come so great a change?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who hath softened down the harshness of their spirit?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who corrected that which was incurable?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see how straightway from the beginning he alarmed them, by laying first, for a foundation, his words concerning hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he spake not of the usual topics: “Who hath warned you to flee from wars, from the inroads of the barbarians, from captivities, from famines, from pestilences?” but concerning another sort of punishment, never before made manifest to them, he was striking the first preparatory note, saying thus, “Who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And full well did he likewise call them, “generation of vipers.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that animal too is said to destroy the mother that is in travail with her, and eating through her belly, thus to come forth unto light; which kind of thing these men also did being “murderers of fathers, and murderers of mothers,”and destroying their instructors with their own hands.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, he stops not at the rebuke, but introduces advice also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Bring forth,” says he, “fruits meet for repentance.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to flee from wickedness is not enough, but you must show forth also great virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For let me not have that contradictory yet ordinarycase, thatrefraining yourselves for a little while, ye return unto the same wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For we are not come for the same objects as the prophets before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, the things that are now are changed, and are more exalted, forasmuch as the Judge henceforth is coming, His very self, the very Lord of the kingdom, leading unto greater self-restraint, calling us to heaven, and drawing us upward to those abodes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause do I unfold the doctrine also touching hell, because both the good things and the painful are for ever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do not therefore abide as ye are, neither bring forward the accustomed pleas, Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, the noble race of your ancestors.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things he said, not as forbidding them to say that they were sprung from those holy men, but as forbidding them to put confidence in this, while they were neglecting the virtue of the soul; at once bringing forward publicly what was in their minds, and foretelling things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because after this they are found to say, “We have Abraham to our father, and were never in bondage to any man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since then it was this, which most of all lifted them up with pride and ruined them, he first puts it down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see how with his honor paid to the patriarch he combines his correction touching these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Namely, having said, “Think not to say, We have Abraham to our father,” he said not, “for the patriarch shall not be able to profit you anything,” but somehow in a more gentle and acceptable manner he intimated the self-same thing, by saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For God is able of these stones to raise up children to Abraham.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now some say, that concerning the Gentiles he saith these things, calling them stones, metaphorically; but I say, that the expression hath also another meaning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But of what kind is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Think not, saith he, that if you should perish, you would make the patriarch childless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is not, this is not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For with God it is possible, both out of stones to give him men, and to bring them to that relationship; since at the beginning also it was so done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was like the birth of men out of stones, when a child came forth from that hardened womb.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This accordingly the prophet also was intimating, when he said, “Look unto the hard rock, whence ye are hewn, and to the hole of the pit, whence ye are digged: look unto Abraham your father, and unto Sarah that bare you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now of this prophecy, you see, he reminds them, showing that if at the beginning he made him a father, as marvellously as if he had made him so out of stones, it was possible for this now also to come to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see how he both alarms them, and cuts them off: in that he said not, “He had already raised up,” lest they should despair of themselves, but that He “is able to raise up:” and he said not, “He is able out of stones to make men,” but what was a much greater thing, “kinsmen and children of Abraham.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how for the time he drew them off from their vain imagination about things of the body, and from their refuge in their forefathers; in order that they might rest the hope of their salvation in their own repentance and continence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how by casting out their carnal relationship, he is bringing in that which is of faith?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Mark then how by what follows also he increases their alarm, and adds intensity to their agonizing fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For having said that “God is able of these stones to raise up children unto Abraham,” he added, “And now also the axe is laid unto the root of the trees,”by all means making his speech alarming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as he from his way of life had much freedom of speech, so they needed his severe rebuke, having been left barrennow for a long time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “why do I say” (such are his words) “that ye are on the point of falling away from your relationship to the patriarch and of seeing others, even those that are of stones, brought in to your pre-eminence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, not to this point only will your penalty reach, but your punishment will proceed further.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For now,” saith he, “the axe is laid unto the root of the trees.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is nothing more terrible than this turn of his discourse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is no longer “a flying sickle,”nor “the taking down of a hedge,” nor “the treading under foot of the vineyard;”but an axe exceeding sharp, and what is worse, it is even at the doors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For inasmuch as they continually disbelieved the prophets, and used to say, “Where is the day of the Lord:”and “let the counsel of the Holy One of Israel come, that we may know it,”by reason that it was many years before what they said came to pass; to lead them off from this encouragement also, he sets the terrors close to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this he declared by saying “now,” and by his putting it to “the root.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For the space between is nothing now,” saith he, “but it is laid to the very root.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he said not, “to the branches,” nor “to the fruits,” but “to the root.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Signifying, that if they were negligent, they would have incurable horrors to endure, and not have so much as a hope of remedy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It being no servant who is now come, as those before Him were, but the very Lord of all, bringing on them His fierce and most effectual vengeance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet, although he hath terrified them again, he suffers them not to fall into despair; but as before he said not “He hath raised up,” but “He is able to raise up children to Abraham” (at once both alarming and comforting them); even so here also he did not say that “it hath touched the root,” but “it is laid to the root, and is now hard by it, and shows signs of no delay.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, even though He hath brought it so near, He makes its cutting depend upon you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if ye change and become better men, this axe will depart without doing anything; but if ye continue in the same ways, He will tear up the tree by the roots.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And therefore, observe, it is neither removed from the root, nor applied as it is doth it cut at all: the one, that ye may not grow supine, the other to let you know that it is possible even in a short time to be changed and saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore he doth also from all topics heighten their fear, thoroughly awakening and pressing them on to repentance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus first their falling away from their forefathers; next, others being introduced instead; lastly, those terrors being at their doors, the certainty of suffering incurable evils (both which he declared by the root and the axe), was sufficient to rouse thoroughly those even that were very supine, and to make them full of anxiety.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I may add, that Paul too was setting forth the same, when he said, “A short wordwill the Lord make upon the whole world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But be not afraid; or rather, be afraid, but despair not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thou hast yet a hope of change; the sentence is not quite absolute,neither did the axe come to cut (else what hindered it from cutting, close as it was to the root?); but on purpose by this fear to make thee a better man, and to prepare thee to bring forth fruit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause he added, “Therefore every tree, which bringeth not forth good fruit, is hewn down, and cast into the fire.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now by the word “every,” he rejects again the privilege which they had from their noble descent; “Why, if thou be Abraham’s own descendant,” saith he, “if thou have thousands of patriarchs to enumerate, thou wilt but undergo a double punishment, abiding unfruitful.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By these words he alarmed even publicans, the soldiers’ mind was startled by him, not casting them into despair, yet ridding them of all security.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For along with the terror, there is also much encouragement in what he saith; since by the expression, “which bringeth not forth good fruit,” he signified that what bears fruit is delivered from all vengeance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And how,” saith one, “shall we be able to bring forth fruit, when the edge is being applied, and the time so strait, and the appointed season cut short.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Thou wilt be able,” saith he, “for this fruit is not of the same kind as that of common trees, waiting a long time, and in bondage to the necessitiesof seasons, and requiring much other management; but it is enough to be willing, and the tree at once hath put forth its fruit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not the nature of the root only, but also the skill of the husbandman contributes the most to that kind of fruit-bearing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For (let me add) on account of this,—lest they should say, “Thou art alarming and pressing, and constraining us, applying an axe, and threatening us with being cut down, yet requiring produce in time of punishment,”—he hath added, to signify the ease of bearing that fruit, “I indeed baptize you with water, but He that cometh after me is mightier than I, the latchet of whose shoe I am not worthy to unloose; He shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire:”implying hereby that considerationonly is needed and faith, not labors and toils; and as it is easy to be baptized, so is it easy to be converted, and to become better men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So having stirred their mind by the fear of God’s judgment, and the expectation of His punishment, and by the mention of the axe, and by the loss of their ancestors, and by the bringing in of those other children, and by the double vengeance of cutting off and burning, and having by all means softened their hardness, and brought them to desire deliverance from so great evils; then he brings in what he hath to say touching Christ; and not simply, but with a declaration of His great superiority.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then in setting forth the difference between himself and Him, lest he should seem to say this out of favor, he establishes the fact by comparison of the gifts bestowed by each of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he did not at once say, “I am not worthy to unloose the latchet of His shoe;” but when he had first set forth the little value of his own baptism, and had shown that it hath nothing more than to lead them to repentance (for he did not say with water of remission, but of repentance), he sets forth Christ’s also, which is full of the unspeakable gift.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus he seems to say, “Lest, on being told that He cometh after me, thou shouldest despise Him as having come later; learn thou the virtueof His gift, and thou wilt clearly know that I uttered nothing worthy nor great, when I said, “I am not worthy to unloose the latchet of His shoe.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So too when thou art told, “He is mightier than I,” do not think I said this in the way of making a comparison.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I am not worthy to be ranked so much as among His servants, no, not even the lowest of His servants, nor to receive the least honored portion of His ministry.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He did not merely say, “His shoes,” but not even “the latchet,” which kind of office was counted the last of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then to hinder thy attributing what he had said to humility, he adds also the proof from the facts: “For He shall baptize you,” saith he, “with the Holy Ghost and with fire.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how great is the wisdom of the Baptist? how, when He Himself is preaching, He saith everything to alarm, and fill them with anxiety; but when He is sending men to Him, whatever was mild and apt to recover them: not bringing forward the axe, nor the tree that is cut down and burnt, and cast into the fire, nor the wrath to come, but remission of sins, and removing of punishment, and righteousness, and sanctification, and redemption, and adoption, and brotherhood, and a partaking of the inheritance, and an abundant supply of the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For all these things he obscurely denoted, when he said, “He shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost;” at once, by the very figure of speech, declaring the abundance of the grace (for he said not, “He will give you the Holy Ghost,” but “He will baptize you with the Holy Ghost”); and by the specification of fire on the other hand indicating the vehement and uncontrollable quality of His grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Imagine only what sort of men it was meet for the hearers to become, when they considered that they were at once to be like the prophets, and like those great ones.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was on this account, you see, that he made mention at all of fire; that he might lead them to reflect on the memory of those men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because, of all the visions that appeared unto them, I had almost said, the more part appeared in fire; thus God discoursed with Moses in the bush, thus with all the people in the mount Sinai, thus with Ezekiel on the cherubim.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark again how he rouses the hearer, by putting that first which was to take place after all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the Lamb was to be slain, and sin to be blotted out, and the enmity to be destroyed, and the burial to take place, and the resurrection, and then the Spirit to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But none of these things doth he mention as yet, but that first which was last, and for the sake of which all the former were done, and which was fittest to proclaim His dignity; so that when the hearer should be told that he was to receive so great a Spirit he might search with himself, how and in what manner this shall be, while sin so prevails; that finding him full of thought and prepared for that lesson, he might thereupon introduce what he had to say touching the Passion, no man being any more offended, under the expectation of such a gift.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore he again cried out, saying, “Behold the Lamb of God, which beareth the sin of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He did not say, “which remitteth,” but, that which implies a more guardian care, “which heareth it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is not all one, simply to remit, and to take it upon Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the one was to be done without peril, the other with death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, he said, “He is Son of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not even this declared His rank openly to the hearers (for they did not so much as know yet how to conceive of Him as a true Son): but by so great a gift of the Spirit that also was established.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore the Father also in sending John gave him, as you know, this as a first token of the dignity of Him that was come, saying, “Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending and remaining, the same is He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore himself too saith, “I saw and bare record that this is the Son of God;” as though the one were to all time the clear evidence of the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, as having uttered the gentler part of his message, and soothed and relaxed the hearer, he again binds him up, that he may not become remiss.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such was the nature of the Jewish nation; by all encouraging things they were easily puffed up, and corrupted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore he again adduces his terrors, saying, “Whose fan is in His hand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, as before he had spoken of the punishment, so here he points out the Judge likewise, and introduces the eternal vengeance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “He will burn the chaff,” saith he, “with unquenchable fire.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou seest that He is Lord of all things, and that He is Himself the Husbandman; albeit in another place He calls His Father the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “My Father,” saith He, “is the Husbandman.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, inasmuch as He had spoken of an axe, lest thou shouldest suppose that the thing needed labor, and the separation was hard to make; by another comparison he suggests the easiness of it, implying that all the world is His; since He could not punish those who were not His own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the present, it is true, all are mingled together (for though the wheat appears gleaming through, yet it lies with the chaff, as on a threshing floor, not as in a garner), but then, great will be the separation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Where now are they by whom hell-fireis disbelieved?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since surely here are two points laid down, one, that He will baptize with the Holy Ghost, the other, that He will burn up the disobedient.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If then that is credible, so is this too, assuredly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, this is why the two predictions are put by him in immediate connection, that by that which hath taken place already, he might accredit the other, as yet unaccomplished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For Christ too Himself in many places doth so, often of the same things, and often of opposites, setting down two prophecies; the one of which He performs here, the other He promises in the future; that such as are too contentious may, from the one which has already come to pass, believe the other also, which is not yet accomplished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For instance, to them that strip themselves of all that they have for His sake He promised to give an hundred fold in the present world, and life eternal in that which is to come; by the things already given making the future also credible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which, as we see, John likewise hath done in this place; laying down two things, that He shall both baptize with the Holy Ghost, and burn up with unquenchable fire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now then, if He had not baptized with the Spirit the apostles, and all every day who are willing, thou mightest have doubts concerning those other things too; but if that which seems to be greater and more difficult, and which transcends all reason, hath been done, and is done every day; how deniest thou that to be true, which is easy, and comes to pass according to reason?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus having said, “He shall baptize with the Holy Ghost and with fire,” and having thence promised great blessings; lest thou, released wholly from the former things, grow supine, he hath added the fan, and the judgment thereby declared.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, “think not at all,” saith he, “that your baptism suffices, if ye become ordinary personshereafter:” for we need both virtue, and plenty of that known self-restraint.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore as by the axe he urges them unto grace, and unto the font, so after grace he terrifies them by the fan, and the unquenchable fire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And of the one sort, those yet unbaptized, he makes no distinction, but saith in general, “Every tree that bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down,”punishing all the unbelievers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whereas after baptism He works out a kind of division, because many of them that believed would exhibit a life unworthy of their faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let no man then become chaff, let no one be tossed to and fro, nor lie exposed to wicked desires, blown about by them easily every way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou continue wheat, though temptation be brought on thee, thou wilt suffer nothing dreadful; nay, for in the threshing floor, the wheels of the car, that are like saws,do not cut in pieces the wheat; but if thou fall away into the weakness of chaff, thou wilt both here suffer incurable ills, being smitten of all men, and there thou wilt undergo the eternal punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For all such persons both before that furnace become food for the irrational passions here, as chaff is for the brute animal: and there again they are material and food for the flame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now to have said directly that He will judge men’s doings, would not so effectually procure acceptance for His doctrine: but to blend with it the parable, and so establish it all, was apter to persuade the hearer, and attract him by a more ample encouragement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also Christ Himselffor the most part so discourses with them; threshing floor, and harvest, and vineyard, and wine-press, and field, and net, and fishing, and all things familiar, and among which they were busied He makes ingredients in His discourses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This kind of thing then the Baptist likewise did here, and offered an exceeding great demonstration of his words, the giving of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “He who hath so great power, as both to forgive sins, and to give the Spirit, much more will these things also be within His power:” so he speaks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how now in due order the mysterycame to be laid as a foundation, before the resurrection and judgment?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And wherefore,” it may be said, “did he not mention the signs and wonders which were straightway to be done by Him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because this was greater than all, and for its sake all those were done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, in his mention of the chief thing, he comprehended all; death dissolved, sins abolished, the curse blotted out, those long wars done away; our entrance into paradise,our ascent into heaven, our citizenship with the angels, our partaking of the good things to come: for in truth this is the earnest of them all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that in mentioning this, he hath mentioned also the resurrection of our bodies, and the manifestation of His miracles here, and our partaking of His kingdom, and the good things, which “eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For all these things He bestowed on us by that gift.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was therefore superfluous to speak of the signs that were immediately to ensue, and which sight can judge of; but those were meet to be discoursed on, whereof they doubted; as for instance, that He is the Son of God; that He exceeds John beyond comparison; that He “beareththe sin of the world;” that He will require an account of all that we do; that our interests are not limited to the present, but elsewhere every one will undergo the due penalty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these things were not as yet proveable by sight.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, knowing these things, let us use great diligence, while we are in the threshing floor; for it is possible while we are here, to change even out of chaff into wheat, even as on the other hand many from wheat have become chaff.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then be supine, nor be carried about with every wind; neither let us separate ourselves from our brethren, though they seem to be small and mean; forasmuch as the wheat also compared with the chaff is less in measure, but better in nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Look not therefore to the forms of outward pomp, for they are prepared for the fire, but to this godly humility, so firm and indissoluble, and which cannot be cut, neither is burnt by the fire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It being for their sake that He bears long with the very chaff, that by their intercourse with them they may become better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore judgment is not yet, that we may be all crowned together, that from wickedness many may be converted unto virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us tremble then at hearing this parable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed that fire is unquenchable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And how,” it may be said, “is it unquenchable?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou not this sun ever burning, and never quenched? didst thou not behold the bush burning, and not consumed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If then thou also desirest to escape the flame, lay up alms beforehand, and so thou wilt not even taste of that fire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if, while here, thou wilt believe what is told thee, thou shalt not so much as see this furnace, after thy departure into that region; but if thou disbelieve it now, thou shalt know it there full well by experience, when no sort of escape is possible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since in truth no entreaty shall avert the punishment from them who have not shown forth an upright life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For believing surely is not enough, since even the devils tremble at God, but for all that they will be punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore our care of our conduct hath need to be great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, this is the very reason of our continually assembling you here; not simply that ye should enter in, but that ye should also reap some fruit from your continuance here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if ye come indeed constantly, but go away again reaping no fruit from thence, ye will have no advantage from your entering in and attendance in this place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if we, when sending children to teachers, should we see them reaping no benefit thereby, begin to be severe in blaming the teachers, and remove them often to others; what excuse shall we have for not bestowing upon virtue even so much diligence as upon these earthly things, but forever bringing our tablets home empty?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet our teachers here are more in number and greater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For no less than prophets and apostles and patriarchs, and all righteous men, are by us set over you as teachers in every Church.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not even so is there any profit, but if you have joined in chanting two or three Psalms, and making the accustomed prayers at random and anyhow, are so dismissed, ye think this enough for your salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Have ye not heard the prophet, saying (or rather God by the prophet), “This people honoreth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, lest this be our case too, wipe thou out the letters, or rather the impressions, which the devil hath engraven in thy soul; and bring me a heart set free from worldly tumults, that without fear I may write on it what I will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since now at least there is nothing else to discern, except his letters;—rapines, covetings, envy, jealousy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore of course, when I receive your tablets, I am not able so much as to read them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I find not the letters, which we every Lord’s day inscribe on you, and so let you go; but others, instead of these, unintelligible and misshapen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, when we have blotted them out, and have written those which are of the Spirit, ye departing, and giving up your hearts to the works of the devil, give him again power to substitute his own characters in you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then will be the end of all this, even without any words of mine, each man’s own conscience knoweth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I indeed will not cease to do my part, and to write in you the right letters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if ye mar our diligence, for our part our reward is unaltered, but your danger is not small.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now, though I would fain say nothing to disgust you, yet I beseech again and entreat you,imitate at least the little children’s diligence in these matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so they first learn the form of the letters, after that they practise themselves in distinguishing them put out of shape, and then at last in their reading they proceed orderly by means of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Just so let us also do; let us divide virtue, and learn first not to swear, nor to forswear ourselves, nor to speak evil; then proceeding to another row,not to envy, not to lust, not to be gluttonous, not to be drunken, not fierce, not slothful, so that from these we may pass on again to the things of the Spirit, and practise continence, and neglect of the belly, temperance, righteousness, to be above glory, and gentle and contrite in mind; and let us join these one with another, and write them upon our soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And all these let us practise at home, with our own friends, with our wife, with our children.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, for the present, let us begin with the things that come first, and are easier; as for instance, with not swearing; and let us practise this one letter continually at home.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, in truth, there are many at home to hinder this our practice; sometimes a man’s servant provoking him, sometimes his wife annoying and angering him, sometimes an indocile and disorderly child urges him on to threatening and swearing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If now at home, when thus continually galled, thou shouldest attain not to be tempted into swearing, thou wilt in the market-place also have power with ease to abide unconquered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, and in like sort, thou wilt attain to keep thyself from insulting any, by not insulting thy wife, nor thy servants, nor any one else among those in thy house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For a man’s wife too not seldom, praising this or that person, or bemoaning herself, stirs him up to speak evil of that other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But do not thou let thyself be constrained to speak evil of him that is praised, but bear it all nobly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if thou shouldest perceive thy servants praising other masters, be not perturbed, but stand nobly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let thy home be a sort of lists, a place of exercise for virtue, that having trained thyself well there, thou mayest with entire skill encounter all abroad.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do this with respect to vainglory also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou train thyself not to be vainglorious in company of thy wife and thy servants, thou wilt not ever afterwards be easily caught by this passion with regard to any one else.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though this malady be in every case grievous and tyrannical, yet is it so especially when a woman is present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If we therefore in that instance put down its power, we shall easily master it in the other cases also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And with respect to the other passions too, let us do this self-same thing, exercising ourselves against them at home, and anointing ourselves every day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that our exercise may be easier, let us further enact a penalty for ourselves, upon our transgressing any of our purposes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And let the very penalty again be such as brings with it not loss, but reward,—such as procures some very great gain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is so, if we sentence ourselves to intenser fastings, and to sleeping often on the bare ground, and to other like austerity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in this way will much profit come unto us from every quarter; we shall both live the sweet life of virtue here, and we shall attain unto the good things to come and be perpetually friends of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But in order that the same may not happen again,—that ye may not, having here admired what is said, go your way, and cast aside at random, wherever it may chance, the tablet of your mind, and so allow the devil to blot out these things;—let each one, on returning home, call his own wife, and tell her these things, and take her to help him; and from this day let him enter into that noble school of exercise, using for oil the supply of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And though thou fall once, twice, many times in thy training, despair not, but stand again, and wrestle; and do not give up until thou hast bound on thee the glorious crown of triumph over the devil, and hast for the time to come stored up the riches of virtue in an inviolable treasure-house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou shouldest establish thyself in the habits of this noble self-restraint, then, not even when remiss, wilt thou be able to transgress any of the commandments, habit imitating the solidity of nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, as to sleep is easy, and to eat, and to drink, and to breathe, so also will the deeds of virtue be easy to us, and we shall reap to ourselves that pure pleasure, resting in a harbor without a wave, and enjoying continual calm, and with a great freight bringing our vessel into haven, in that City, on that day; and we shall attain unto the undecaying crowns, unto which may we all attain, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be all glory and might, now and always, and world without end.
